project: Lever Money - Baixa Imediata + Status pending_ca
description: Duas mudancas: (1) Confirmar que baixas rodam imediatamente apos sync de payments no nightly pipeline e onboarding backfill, sem scheduler standalone. (2) Introduzir status 'pending_ca' para payments de sellers sem config CA, permitindo reprocessamento quando o seller migrar para dashboard_ca.

userStories.title: Processor: new pending_ca status for sellers without CA config
userStories.description: As the system, when the nightly pipeline processes payments for sellers missing CA configuration (ca_conta_bancaria, ca_centro_custo_variavel), I want to save them with status='pending_ca' instead of 'skipped', so the onboarding backfill knows to reprocess them later.
acceptanceCriteria: In processor.py, when get_missing_ca_launch_fields() returns missing fields, the payment is saved with status='pending_ca' (not 'skipped')
The error field is still populated with 'missing_ca_config:...' for traceability
processor_fee and processor_shipping are computed via _extract_processor_charges() and saved in the upsert even for pending_ca payments
raw_payment (jsonb) continues to be saved in the upsert (ML data preserved)
The existing flow for sellers WITH CA config is unchanged (approved -> queued as before)
Lint passes (ruff check app/services/processor.py)
userStories.notes: Key code location: processor.py lines 224-236, the early return block after get_missing_ca_launch_fields(). Also update _upsert_payment call to include processor_fee and processor_shipping params.

userStories.title: Daily sync: treat pending_ca as reprocessable
userStories.description: As the system, the daily sync (nightly step 1) must handle payments that already exist as pending_ca in Supabase correctly — they should be reprocessed if the seller now has CA config.
acceptanceCriteria: In daily_sync.py sync_seller_payments(), the existing_orders dict is loaded for all payments including those with status='pending_ca'
The should_reprocess logic treats pending_ca as reprocessable: if a payment exists with processor_status='pending_ca', should_reprocess is set to True
If the seller still lacks CA config, the processor saves as pending_ca again (idempotent, no error)
If the seller now has CA config (upgraded to dashboard_ca), the processor creates ca_jobs and changes status to queued
Lint passes (ruff check app/services/daily_sync.py)
userStories.notes: Key code location: daily_sync.py lines 340-355, the should_reprocess block. Add 'pending_ca' to the condition that triggers reprocessing. Current code checks ml_status change, status_detail change, and pending/queued processor_status.

userStories.title: Onboarding backfill: exclude pending_ca from already_done set
userStories.description: As the system, when the onboarding backfill runs for a seller recently upgraded to dashboard_ca, payments with status='pending_ca' must be reprocessed (ca_jobs created).
acceptanceCriteria: In onboarding_backfill.py _load_already_done(), the status 'pending_ca' is NOT in the list of statuses considered done
The current list remains: ['synced', 'queued', 'refunded', 'skipped', 'skipped_non_sale'] — 'pending_ca' is simply absent
After reprocessing, the payment status changes from pending_ca to queued (then synced when CaWorker completes)
Lint passes (ruff check app/services/onboarding_backfill.py)
userStories.notes: Key code location: onboarding_backfill.py lines 556-563 in _load_already_done(). The .in_('status', [...]) filter. Just verify 'pending_ca' is not there (it shouldn't be since it doesn't exist yet, but confirm the list is correct and won't accidentally include it).

userStories.title: Verify baixa runs immediately in nightly pipeline (no standalone scheduler)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to confirm that when nightly_pipeline_enabled=true, baixas run as step 5 of the pipeline immediately after sync, and no standalone 10h BRT scheduler is started.
acceptanceCriteria: In app/main.py lifespan, when nightly_pipeline_enabled=true, the standalone _daily_baixa_scheduler is NOT started (verify the conditional logic)
The nightly pipeline function runs _run_baixas_all_sellers() as step 5 sequentially after steps 1-4
The onboarding backfill calls _trigger_baixas() after processing payments and release report (step 7 of its flow) with no time-based dependency
Add a code comment in main.py at the nightly pipeline section noting that baixas run immediately after sync (not on a separate schedule)
Lint passes (ruff check app/main.py)
userStories.notes: This is mostly a verification story. Check app/main.py lifespan function for the conditional that guards _daily_baixa_scheduler startup. The nightly pipeline should already have this right, but verify and add a clarifying comment.

userStories.title: Update documentation for pending_ca status and baixa timing
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the documentation to reflect the new pending_ca status and the baixa timing clarification.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md section 5 (Estrutura do Projeto): payments status enum updated to include pending_ca
docs/TABELAS.md: payments table status field updated to include pending_ca in the enum list
docs/REGRAS_NEGOCIO.md: new section or note documenting pending_ca behavior (when it's set, what it means, how backfill handles it)
docs/FLUXO_DETALHADO.md: nightly pipeline description notes that baixas run immediately after sync (step 5), not on separate schedule
app/services/CLAUDE.md: processor.py description updated to mention pending_ca for sellers without CA config
Lint passes on all modified files
userStories.notes: Documentation files: CLAUDE.md (root), docs/TABELAS.md, docs/REGRAS_NEGOCIO.md, docs/FLUXO_DETALHADO.md, app/services/CLAUDE.md. Keep changes minimal and factual.
